name: Transit
content: Huntsville has a semi-usable bus network that operates on a hub and spoke system, except that the only hub is boxed in by freeways, the buses only run every half hour at best, and only the west side of the city receives bus service. Oh, and there was that one time there was a pile of Number 2 on one of the seats of the Number 2 line bus. There is some intercity bus service through Greyhound, though the station is located far from the city center. There is no bus service to the airport, which is also located far from the city center. Most of the distances in Huntsville are too far to walk. While some areas of Huntsville are ok for biking, I would only recommend biking as your main mode of transportation if you are an intermediate to advanced urban biker (meaning comfortable with curb hopping, weaving through traffic, and asserting yourself on the road). Huntsville is shackled by the automobile, and I have seen many car crashes in the area.
